steps.title: Frame though as parenthetical
steps.content: The first clause says plants like Platanares supply only a small fraction of Honduras's electricity. The word though is used parenthetically before a noun phrase explaining the limiting scarcity.

steps.title: Set off though
steps.content: The matching punctuation places commas on both sides of parenthetical though. Choice A and Choice B use semicolons even though the following words are not an independent clause, and Choice C lacks the second comma. The answer is D.
